title: Roasted Uruguayan Kale Empanadas
description: A traditional Uruguayan dish featuring roasted kale and flaky pastry, perfect for a solo dinner, and is egg-free friendly. This recipe reduces the complexity of traditional empanadas while maintaining the authentic flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the kale with olive oil, salt, and black pepper until the kale is evenly coated.
Spread the kale mixture on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 15 minutes, or until the kale is tender and slightly caramelized.
In a large skillet, saute the sliced onion and minced garlic over medium heat until the onion is translucent.
Add the roasted kale to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and garlic.
Roll out the empanada pastry on a floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Spoon about 1/4 cup of the kale mixture onto the center of each pastry circle.
Brush the edges of the pastry with water and fold the pastry in half to form a half-moon shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with a little bit of water.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
